title: Human breast cancer patient tissue dissociation for single-cell RNA sequencing
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"5cvui","text":"Efficient and gentle dissociation of solid tumors is essential for preserving cell viability and transcriptomic integrity in single-cell RNA sequencing workflows. This protocol outlines an optimized approach for processing fresh human breast cancer tissue, designed to minimize cell stress while maximizing viable cell recovery. By combining enzymatic digestion with mechanical dissociation, the method yields high-quality single-cell suspensions with consistently high viability and strong transcript capture, enabling reliable downstream analysis of the tumor microenvironment at single-cell resolution.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
guidelines: {"blocks":[{"key":"94v4e","text":"This protocol is optimized for the dissociation of fresh human breast cancer tissue to generate high-quality single-cell suspensions suitable for RNA sequencing. When performed promptly and under proper conditions, the method yields high cell viability and robust transcript capture, resulting in high UMI and gene counts per cell.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":51,"length":32}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"4uqir","text":"Breast tumor specimens are collected fresh from the operating room and immediately submerged in MACS Tissue Storage Solution to preserve cell integrity during transport on ice. Timely processing upon arrival in the laboratory is critical to maintaining viability and ensuring high-quality single-cell RNA sequencing results.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":96,"length":28}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"433pb","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"5th3l","text":"Manuscript citation","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"nbdc","text":"Please remember to cite the following publication along with this protocol","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"1il55","text":"Single-cell RNA sequencing reveals different cellular states in malignant cells and the tumor microenvironment in primary and metastatic ER-positive breast cancer. Furkan Ozmen, Tugba Y. Ozmen, Aysegul Ors, Mahnaz Janghorban, Matthew J. Rames, Xi Li, Aaron Reid Doe, Fariba Behbod, Gordon B. Mills, Hisham Mohammed NPJ Breast Cancer (2025)","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"9k8ts","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
before_start:
{"blocks":[{"key":"q07u","text":"Before You Start

This protocol is optimized for single-cell isolation from fresh human tumor biopsy cores using 10x Genomics 3’ v3.1 chemistry. Before beginning, ensure that:

Sample integrity is maintained by keeping tissue on ice at all times before dissociation.
Sterile technique is used throughout to prevent contamination and ensure sample quality.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":16},{"style":"bold","offset":76,"length":30},{"style":"bold","offset":113,"length":30},{"style":"bold","offset":177,"length":30},{"style":"bold","offset":226,"length":19},{"style":"bold","offset":267,"length":17}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"ergf9","text":"All necessary sterile consumables and reagents prepared in advance.
A ROCK inhibitor stock (10 mM Y-27632) is pre-aliquoted and stored at −80°C.
Pre-aliquated dissociation enzymes and tubes are thawed.
Dissociation media is prepared fresh just prior to the dissociation step.
All centrifuges and incubators are set to appropriate temperatures (e.g., 4°C for centrifuge, 37°C for incubation).
The EasySep magnet and dead cell/RBC removal reagents, sterile scissors/tweezer sets are readily accessible and brought to room temperature as required.
Cell counter (Countess) is calibrated, clean, and functional.
","type":"header-three","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":14,"length":32},{"style":"bold","offset":70,"length":36},{"style":"bold","offset":158,"length":31},{"style":"bold","offset":202,"length":18},{"style":"bold","offset":276,"length":30},{"style":"bold","offset":396,"length":80}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"c597r","text":" ","type":"atomic","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[{"key":0,"offset":0,"length":1}],"data":{}},{"key":"9rcpo","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{"0":{"type":"notes","mutability":"MUTABLE","data":"‱ Note: This protocol is optimized for small biopsy cores (≤2 mm). For larger chunks or solid tumors, dissociation time and filtering steps may require adjustments. Refer to notes throughout the procedure for alternate handling of large specimens."}}}

steps: This protocol is optimized for small biopsy cores. Adjust for big tumor chunks.
Phenol red free RPMI, +/- 5% CS-FBS (charcoal stripped) Gibco - Thermo Fisher Scientific Catalog # A338210
Dulbeccos Phosphate-Buffered Saline (DPBS), No Calcium, No Magnesium Gibco - Thermo Fisher Scientific Catalog #14190144
Micro Dissecting Scissors 3.5 Straight Sharp (RS-5910) (Roboz Surgical Store, RS-5910)
(Biology Tweezer 115 mm, Ideal Tak, 7SG.CX.0, Biology Tweezer 115 mm (Ideal Tak Chiasso, Switzerland). Manufacturer Part Number: 7SG.CX.0, https://s3.amazonaws.com/pr-journal/6r928n6.jpg )
Falcon™ Standard Tissue Culture Dishes ( Falcon™ Standard Tissue Culture Dishes, 08-772E)
5ml Eppendorf Tubes (5ml Eppendorf Tubes, 14-282-301)
Falcon Round-Bottom (FACS) Tubes with Cell Strainer Cap (08-771-23) or 40μm cell strainer (07-201-430) (Falcon Round-Bottom (FACS) Tubes with Cell Straine, 08-771-23)
EasySep Dead Cell Removal (Annexin V) Kit (17899) STEMCELL Technologies Inc. Catalog #17899
Y-27632 (ROCK inhibitor) (Selleck Chemicals Catalog # S1049) Selleckchem Catalog #S1049
gentleMACs human dissociation enzymes (130-095-929) Miltenyi Biotec Catalog #130-095-929
EasySep RBC Depletion Reagent (18170) STEMCELL Technologies Inc. Catalog #18170
GentleMACS™ Octo Dissociator with Heaters (Dissociator with Heaters, Miltenyi Biotec, 130-134-029, https://www.miltenyibiotec.com/US-en/products/gentlemacs-octo-dissociator-with-heaters.html )
EasySep Magnet (18000) STEMCELL Technologies Inc. Catalog #18000
Rinse the tissue in DPBS in a sterile plastic petri dish.
Keep sample on ice until ready for dissociation.
Place the tube containing the DEAD cell removal+ RBC Depletion reagent in a magnet and incubate for 5min.
Add 100μL/mL of original sample volume of RBC Depletion Reagent to the 2.5ml mix.
Add DPBS+2%CS-FBS to top up the sample to 2.5ml. Mix by gently pipetting up and down 2-3 times
Vortex the RapidSpheres and add 100μL/mL of sample volume of RapidSpheres and mix gently.
Gently mix by tapping the tube, incubate @RT for 3 minutes
Add 100μL/mL of sample volume of Biotin Selection Cocktail to sample.

Depending on tissue quality and appearance, dead cell and RBC removal can be performed in a combined or separate step. If the sample is particularly bloody, necrotic, or has low viability , it is recommended to perform sequential magnetic binding steps to improve removal efficiency and downstream cell quality.
Spin at 4 degrees 300g for 7 min to collect cells
Wash the tube C with RPMI+2% CS-FBS to collect any residual cells and pass through the same strainer to wash the strainer as well. Transfer to a FACS tube if performing DEAD/RBC magnetic removal.
Filter the dissociated cells+dissociation media through a 70μm cell strainer.

After 20-25 min, gently pipette the tissue (4-5 times). For most biopsy cores, they will be completely dissociated after 20-25 min and with gentle pipetting. If there are still undissociated pieces after pipetting, run h_tumor_02 program on the gentleMACs dissociator. If still see big chunks, incubate for another 5-10 minutes.
Incubate at 37°C on a bench incubator with rocker, gently rocking the media (speed 190) for 20-25 min. (Place the tube at a 45-degree angle while rocking).
Run h_tumor_02 program on the gentleMACs dissociator. (for very hard tumors use h_tumor_01). Spin for short (5s) to collect media+cell at the bottom of the tube.
While holding the Tube C tilted and maintaining media at the bottom of the tube, mince cells with a sharp scissor on the neck of the Tube C. Close the cap and mix well gently.
Add 2ml of phenol red-free RPMI to a Tube C with the cocktail of enzymes H+A+R plus Rock inhibitor (final 10uM).
For biopsy cores which are small additional incubation is not required. Mash the chunks on the strainer and they’ll dissociate and pass through the strainer.
For Big and multiple cores: filter through a 40μm cell strainer in a 50ml tube. Mash the undissociated chunks with a 5ml syringe plunger.
For small cores, filter through a FACS Tubes with Cell Strainer Cap. Mash the undissociated chunks with a 1ml syringe plunger.
Add 100μL/mL of sample volume of Dead Cell Removal (Annexin V) Cocktail to sample (20μL for 200μL of sample).
If there are still RBC or dead cells after the spin and count, repeat DEAD+RBCC removal one more time and incubate in the magnet for 5min to increase the purity.
Adjust volume so that there is 700-1,200 cells/μl for 10X Genomics 3’ v3.1 protocol.
Pick up the magnet, and in one continuous motion invert the magnet and tube, pouring the cell suspension into a new 5ml tube.
Count 3 times on the countess and average the count.

Decant the media gently without disturbing the pellet. While still holding the tube upside down dry the tube rim with a Kimwipe. Add DPBS+2%CS-FBS (total of ~200μl liquid in the tube with the leftover wash media).
Load 20,000 cells using the Chromium Next GEM Single Cell 3-prime kit v3.1, onto a 10x Chip G and process according to manufacturer's protocol (CG000315 Rev A).
Chromium Controller (microfuidic based single-cell capture, 10x Genomics, 1000171, https://www.10xgenomics.com/instruments/chromium-controller )
Chromium Next GEM Single Cell 3-prime Kit v3.1 10x Genomics Catalog #1000268
Chromium Next GEM Chip G Kit 10x Genomics Catalog #1000120
creator: Furkan Ozmen
